items.headline: Yankees shortstop Caballero sidelined with finger injury, hopes to avoid injured list
items.description:
BALTIMORE (AP) — New York Yankees shortstop José Caballero has been sidelined with a sore finger and will undergo tests to determine the extent of the injury.
Caballero hurt his right middle finger diving back to first base in Sunday's loss to the Milwaukee Brewers. He was not in starting lineup Monday night when the Yankees opened a three-game series against the Orioles.

items.headline: Hull beat Millwall to reach Championship play-off final
items.description: Substitutes Mohamed Belloumi and Joe Gelhardt both scored as Hull beat Millwall 2-0 on Monday to book a place in...

items.headline: Spurs draw with Leeds for priceless point in Premier League survival fight
items.description:
LONDON (AP) — Tottenham Hotspur took a small and potentially crucial step towards retaining its Premier League status after drawing at home with Leeds United 1-1 on Monday.
Spurs, just above the drop zone, moved two points clear of relegation rival West Ham.

items.headline: Veteran tight end David Njoku signs with LA Chargers after 9 seasons with Browns
items.description:
EL SEGUNDO, Calif. (AP) — Veteran tight end David Njoku agreed to a one-year deal with the Los Angeles Chargers on Monday.
Njoku spent the first nine seasons of his NFL career with the Cleveland Browns, earning one Pro Bowl selection while catching 384 passes for 4,062 yards and 34 touchdowns. He is second in Browns franchise history to Ozzie Newsome for catches and TDs among tight ends, but he became a free agent in February.

items.headline: Guardians manager Stephen Vogt misses 2nd game with viral infection
items.description:
CLEVELAND (AP) — Guardians manager Stephen Vogt will miss his second straight game Monday night because of a viral infection.
Bench coach Tony Arnerich, who is Vogt's housemate in Cleveland, will serve as acting manager again when the Guardians host the Los Angeles Angels in the opener of a three-game series.

items.headline: Rangers scratch Nathan Eovaldi from his scheduled start because of left side tightness
items.description:
ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Texas Rangers right-hander Nathan Eovaldi was scratched from his scheduled start Monday night because of left side tightness.
About three hours before the scheduled first pitch of their series opener at home against Arizona, the Rangers said right-handed reliever Jakob Junis would start in Eovaldi's place for what would be a bullpen game.

items.headline: Houston’s Carlos Correa undergoes season-ending surgery for torn left ankle tendon
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — Houston’s Carlos Correa had surgery to repair a torn tendon in his left ankle Monday.
The star infielder was injured Tuesday and said the following day that he would need the season-ending surgery.

items.headline: SEC commissioner Greg Sankey stands firm on 16-team CFP, details challenges amid 24-team push
items.description:
BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Southeastern Conference commissioner Greg Sankey stood firm behind a 16-team College Football Playoff expansion Monday, indicating that a disagreement with the Big Ten — which backs doubling the current bracket to 24 teams — is lingering deep into the offseason.
“That focus hasn’t changed," Sankey said at the APSE Southeast Region meeting at the Alabama Sports Hall of Fame. "We’re open to the conversation, but there are a lot of ideas out there that have to be supported with analysis and information, not speculation.”

items.headline: Dembele again voted player of the year in France
items.description: Paris Saint-Germain forward Ousmane Dembele was on Monday named the Ligue 1 player of the year for the second year running, after leading the club to the brink of a 14th...

items.headline: Braves activate Ha-Seong Kim and place Eli White on concussion injured list
items.description:
ATLANTA (AP) — Atlanta Braves shortstop Ha-Seong Kim was activated from the injured list on Monday, clearing the way for him to make his season debut.
The Braves said they activated the 30-year-old Kim and placed outfielder Eli White on the seven-day concussion injured list.
